untranslatable_reason: This is an options strategy signal (reverse call spread) requiring multi-leg options pricing and Greeks (delta, theta, vega). The signal depends on: (1) establishing a specific options structure with defined strikes and extrinsic values, (2) calculating break-even points dynamically based on premium paid/received, and (3) evaluating P&L at expiration. Standard equity price/technical indicators cannot model options spread pricing, implied volatility decay, or strike-relative profitability. The 'upside break-even point' is not a market indicator but a calculated parameter dependent on option premiums negotiated at entry. Backtesting this requires options pricing engine, not equity signal logic.
translation_notes: Signal describes a reverse call spread (2 long calls at lower strike K1, 1 short call at higher strike K2). Max profit = (K2-K1) × contracts - net debit. Max loss = net debit paid. Profit zone starts at K2 + net debit. This requires options module to: (1) define strikes K1, K2, and net debit from entry, (2) track underlying price relative to break-even at expiration date, (3) calculate P&L including time decay. Cannot be converted to equity-only technical rule set.
